deep_dive.title: OMGTex: One-stage Multi-style Facial Texture Reconstruction without Geometry Guidance
deep_dive.abstract: We propose OMGTex, an end-to-end diffusion-based framework for reconstructing high-quality and editable facial UV textures from multi-style facial images. Existing texture reconstruction methods face two major limitations: (1) Fragility due to reliance on 3D geometry priors, which are difficult to estimate accurately, especially under facial occlusions or in stylized domains; and (2) A lack of semantic disentanglement, inhibiting region-specific texture editing and style transfer. Our work addresses both challenges simultaneously.   Our core innovation is a geometry-free pipeline that directly maps a 2D face image to its corresponding editable UV texture. We introduce two key techniques: First, to address the challenge of UV misalignment common in diffusion generation, we introduce a gradient-guided refinement strategy at inference time, which explicitly corrects structural consistency. Second, we leverage the inherent semantic distribution capability of diffusion models and design a novel training paradigm to enhance this tendency, enabling semantic-aware editing of facial texture. Furthermore, to address the data scarcity in multi-style texture reconstruction, we construct CANVAS, the first comprehensive paired texture reconstruction dataset covering realistic and diverse stylized domains.   To the best of our knowledge, OMGTex is the first geometry-free inference framework that achieves robust, style-consistent, and editable facial texture reconstruction across diverse domains. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple facial texture benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: facial texture reconstruction using diffusion model without geometry
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型的无几何面部纹理重建
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型的无几何面部纹理重建

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes OMGTex, an end-to-end diffusion-based framework for editable facial UV texture reconstruction from multi-style images without 3D geometry priors.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有面部纹理重建依赖3D几何先验，在遮挡和风格化域中脆弱。本文提出OMGTex，一种端到端扩散框架，直接将2D人脸映射为可编辑UV纹理。通过解决UV不对齐和语义解耦，实现了高质量重建和区域编辑能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有面部纹理重建依赖3D几何先验，在遮挡和风格化域中脆弱。本文提出OMGTex，一种端到端扩散框架，直接将2D人脸映射为可编辑UV纹理。通过解决UV不对齐和语义解耦，实现了高质量重建和区域编辑能力。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

quick_skim.title: Veda: Scalable Video Diffusion via Distilled Sparse Attention
quick_skim.abstract: Scaling Diffusion Transformers to generate high-resolution, long videos is constrained by the quadratic cost of self-attention, and existing sparse attention methods degrade under high sparsity. We show empirically that generation quality is determined not by the sparsity ratio itself, but by how well the sparse mask aligns with the tile-wise geometry of full attention. Based on this insight, we propose Veda, a distilled sparse attention framework that formulates tile selection as an explicit reconstruction problem from full attention. Veda integrates statistics-aware tile scoring with head-aware tiling to reduce estimation error and structural mismatch, enabling aggressive sparsity. A hardware-efficient tile-skipping kernel converts theoretical sparsity into practical wall-clock speedups. Experiments on large video diffusion models, including Waver and Wan2.1, demonstrate substantial acceleration with no noticeable degradation in generation quality. To generate 720P 10-second videos on Waver-T2V-12B, Veda achieves a 5.1$\times$ end-to-end speedup and a 10.5$\times$ self-attention speedup, reducing attention overhead from 92% to 50%. Notably, the gains increase with sequence length, indicating that Veda scales favorably with spatiotemporal resolution across models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Sparse attention for scaling video diffusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于扩展视频扩散模型的稀疏注意力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于扩展视频扩散模型的稀疏注意力
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于扩展视频扩散模型的稀疏注意力
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Veda proposes a distilled sparse attention framework for diffusion transformers, enabling high-resolution long video generation with reduced self-attention cost.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Veda，一种蒸馏式稀疏注意力框架，用于扩散Transformer视频生成。通过将图块选择建模为从完整注意力重建的问题，结合统计感知评分和头部感知平铺，在保持生成质量的同时实现高稀疏度，支持高分辨率长视频的高效生成。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出Veda，一种蒸馏式稀疏注意力框架，用于扩散Transformer视频生成。通过将图块选择建模为从完整注意力重建的问题，结合统计感知评分和头部感知平铺，在保持生成质量的同时实现高稀疏度，支持高分辨率长视频的高效生成。

quick_skim.title: Channel-wise Vector Quantization
quick_skim.abstract: We present Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ), a novel image tokenization paradigm that replaces patch-wise tokens with channel-wise tokens. Unlike conventional vector quantization, which assigns a discrete token to each patch feature vector, CVQ quantizes each channel of the feature map. This formulation represents an image as discrete levels of visual details, rather than as a grid of spatial patches. Based on CVQ, we introduce a new visual autoregressive framework with "next-channel prediction". Instead of rendering images patch by patch in raster order, our Channel-wise Autoregressive (CAR) model predicts image channels sequentially, producing progressively enriched visual details. Specifically, it first sketches global structure and then refines fine-grained attributes, akin to a human artist's workflow. Empirically, we show that: (1) CVQ achieves 100% codebook utilization with a 16K+ codebook size without any bells and whistles, and substantially improves reconstruction quality over conventional VQ; and (2) CAR attains a DPG score of 86.7 and a GenEval score of 0.79, demonstrating strong effectiveness for text-to-image generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Novel image tokenization for generative models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 生成模型的新型图像标记化方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 生成模型的新型图像标记化方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 生成模型的新型图像标记化方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Channel-wise Vector Quantization (CVQ) replaces patch-wise tokens with channel-wise tokens, enabling autoregressive generation via next-channel prediction that progressively enriches visual details.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出了通道级向量量化（CVQ），将传统逐块标记化替换为逐通道标记化，将图像表示为视觉细节的离散层级，并基于此提出了通道级自回归（CAR）框架，利用“下一通道预测”逐步生成从全局结构到细粒度属性的图像，类似于画家的创作过程。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出了通道级向量量化（CVQ），将传统逐块标记化替换为逐通道标记化，将图像表示为视觉细节的离散层级，并基于此提出了通道级自回归（CAR）框架，利用“下一通道预测”逐步生成从全局结构到细粒度属性的图像，类似于画家的创作过程。
